suggestions.title: Turn the anchor-lineage rewrite into a 14-slide group-meeting deck
suggestions.description: You just created `non_autoregressive_lm_survey_anchor_lineage.md` and `paper_selection.md` for a 45–60 minute talk, but there is still no slide artifact in the project. Codex can turn the 12-anchor narrative into a concise Chinese deck with speaker notes and figures now.
suggestions.prompt: Read `non_autoregressive_lm_survey_anchor_lineage.md`, `paper_selection.md`, and the relevant notes, then create a 14-slide Chinese group-meeting deck in this project. Use the 12 anchor papers as the backbone, keep the five-category taxonomy, add one slide on the speed/KV-cache caveat, and include brief speaker notes per slide. Output both a Markdown slide script and a `.pptx` if the environment supports it.

suggestions.title: Repair the lone non-autoregressive-nmt repo failure in the manifest

suggestions.prompt: Investigate the `non-autoregressive-nmt` official repo failure from `logs/acquisition_report.md` and `data/papers.yaml`. Re-check primary-source evidence, determine whether the repo moved, was archived, or should be marked unavailable, then update the manifest, note, and acquisition report so this paper no longer has a stale broken official-repo record.

suggestions.title: Write the Q&A crib sheet your group will ask after this survey
suggestions.description: The rewritten survey centers on three arguments that usually trigger pushback: whether XLNet/BERT count as NAR, whether diffusion is really faster than AR, and why block diffusion is not a sixth category. Codex can prepare a short answer sheet tied directly to your anchor survey before the meeting.
suggestions.prompt: Create a compact Chinese Q&A crib sheet for this project based on `non_autoregressive_lm_survey_anchor_lineage.md`. Focus on the likely seminar questions: why BERT/XLNet are bridges not strict NAR models, when diffusion language models are or are not faster than AR, why block diffusion stays a cross-cutting tag instead of a sixth main category, and how category 4 differs from category 5. Keep answers precise, evidence-backed, and ready to speak aloud.

suggestions.title: Create a defense sheet for the terminology traps in this survey
suggestions.description: The fresh anchor-lineage draft leans heavily on distinctions like XLNet vs NAR, CMLM vs masked diffusion, and continuous token states vs learned latent flows. Codex can prepare a compact rebuttal sheet from your local notes so you have crisp answers ready when those predictable lab-meeting questions come up.
suggestions.prompt: Create a concise Q&A defense sheet for this survey using only local project files. Focus on the distinctions people are most likely to challenge in discussion: non-left-to-right vs non-autoregressive, MLM vs full generative model, CMLM vs masked diffusion, MDLM vs LLaDA, AR-to-DLM adaptation vs from-scratch diffusion, and continuous token-state models vs latent/embedding flow models.
